entry.title: NASA-Funded Study Shows How Controlled Burns Help Sequoias Survive Wildfires - NASA Science
entry.summary: A new study, funded by NASA, found that giant sequoias were nearly four times more likely to survive extreme wildfires in forests that have been managed with prescribed burns, which decrease tree mortality.

entry.title: Amendment 64: New Opportunity: A.15 Biosphere - NASA Science
entry.summary: NASA's Biosphere research area focuses on research that provides foundational knowledge on interactions between and within terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems,

entry.title: Acid-resistant nanocage shows promise for targeted gastric cancer therapy
entry.summary: Researchers from the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore (NUS Medicine), have developed a swallowable nanoscale delivery platform designed to transport therapeutic enzymes through the stomach's acidic environment and activate a cancer-killing reaction at tumor sites. Early preclinical findings suggest that the approach may offer a new strategy for treating gastric cancer more precisely while reducing damage to healthy tissue.

entry.title: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration
entry.summary: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of fuel-saving aircraft power systems.

entry.title: Fortnite's latest Sprite trailer features an 'Obsession' crossover – could skins be next?
entry.summary: Fortnite skins based on the film Obsession have become a real possibility following a recent collaboration with the film!

entry.summary: Team Vitality wins a back-to-back MWI at EWC 2026 championship title after defeating NAVI PH in a six-round series.

entry.summary: The Atmosphere research program is soliciting proposals for data analysis and modeling of NASA supported airborne campaign data. NASA’s Earth Science Division

entry.title: How global energy production is changing, in 6 charts
entry.summary: Fossil fuels still dominate global electricity, but wind and solar are surging. In 2025, renewables outpaced coal worldwide for the first time, supplying 34% of electricity.

entry.title: From Hampton to Mars: How NASA Langley Helped Land on the Red Planet - NASA
entry.summary: Fifty years ago, NASA’s Viking 1 and 2 landers made the first successful landings on Mars, opening a new era in planetary exploration. Behind that achievement

entry.title: NVIDIA NVLink: The Scale-Up Network for AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: The demand for AI continues to accelerate. Workloads are getting larger, models are becoming more complex, and there is mounting pressure to deploy AI compute infrastructure faster than ever.

entry.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
entry.summary: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

entry.summary: Developers building 3D, design, simulation, robotics, and industrial digital twin applications need ways to bring physical AI capabilities into the tools and services they already use.

entry.summary: IPTC is the global standards body of the news media. We provide the technical foundation for the news ecosystem.

entry.title: Every major Counter-Strike 2 update, explained
entry.summary: Valve is traditionally slow to release big CS2 updates, but time after time, we receive fundamental changes to the game. They shape the experience for casual and pro players alike.

entry.title: Microsoft expands Azure AI and HPC infrastructure with AMD - The Official Microsoft Blog
entry.summary: AI workloads are scaling faster than any single infrastructure approach can support — with more models, new agent-driven workloads and surging compute demand driving the need for greater specialization across the stack. To meet this need, Microsoft continues to evolve Azure’s infrastructure, including expanding its AI fleet with AMD’s most advanced AI and high-performance computing...

entry.summary: Bad decisions have turned efficient technology into a driver of light pollution. There is a better way

entry.summary: Learn how Explain Your Thinking helps educators use AI to make student reasoning visible and improve feedback, assessment, and intervention.

entry.summary: BGS scientists are contributing to a continent-wide initiative that is strengthening collaboration across borders to tackle shared subsurface challenges and unlock new opportunities.

entry.title: Nanoparticles could remove harmful immune molecules from blood
entry.summary: The immune system, the body's defense network against infections and injuries, can sometimes become too active. In these cases, it can produce too many immune mediators, fragments of genetic material or proteins that regulate immune responses.

entry.summary: If your week flew by — we know ours did — catch up here with what you might have missed. The week at Retraction Watch featured: ‘Disappointed’: Cochrane journal asked researchers to publish article…

entry.title: Restarting Nuclear Power Plants: Preserving and Restoring Value - EPRI Journal
entry.summary: Share this article:Recent growth in electricity demand, expanding carbon-free energy goals, and renewed concerns about energy security are driving utilities to reconsider nuclear power plants that have been permanently shut down or are approaching retirement. EPRI examines the technical, regulatory, and business factors that influence whether these facilities can return to service and what steps […]

entry.title: This Graduate Student Equips NASA’s Robots With Assembly Skills
entry.summary: Her algorithm enables robots to install antennas on satellites

entry.title: Majorities of Americans say key financial milestones are harder for today’s young adults to reach
entry.summary: Most Americans say it’s harder for young adults to find a job, buy a house, pay for college and save for their future than it was for their parents' generation.

entry.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Delivers Mars Flyby Data, Time-lapse Video - NASA Science
entry.summary: When NASA’s Psyche spacecraft flew by Mars for a gravity assist on May 15, 2026, it gave the mission team an opportunity to test instruments and prep for science they’ll conduct when reaching the metal-rich asteroid Psyche in 2029, and collect images and a time-lapse video of the Mars encounter.

entry.title: Thinner wires, faster electrons: Quantum material challenges copper at chip scale
entry.summary: Electrical interconnects may very well be the unsung heroes of modern microchips. These tiny wires—typically made of copper due to its high conductivity—string together the billions of transistors that drive our computers and electronic devices. But as the technology advances and additional transistors are piled on, the components must shrink to the nanoscale. And that's when copper begins to fail.

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
entry.summary: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

entry.summary: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

entry.title: NovoTags: AI-designed proteins help scientists see inside living cells | EMBL
entry.summary: Researchers have designed synthetic and inducible proteins that bind to bright fluorescent dyes with high specificity and affinity, significantly expanding the toolkit for multicolour imaging of proteins inside cells

Part 2: Specifications of AKN 3.1 CS01 is now available for public review and comment before a call for consent as OASIS Standard. Akoma Ntoso defines an XML vocabulary for parliamentary, legislative, and judicial documents, enabling machine-readable exchange […]

entry.title: Graphene nanoribbons survive gamma radiation, revealing potential sensors for fusion reactors
entry.summary: University of Arizona researchers have demonstrated a promising new application for graphene nanoribbons, a nanoscale semiconductor material with the potential to withstand extreme environments. The team's findings could help clear a key hurdle to bringing fusion energy to the electric grid.

entry.title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet - NASA
entry.summary: New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by

entry.title: Is Counter-Strike 2 going soft? Esports fans react to new comms rule and debate hate speech vs. trash talk
entry.summary: A newly-added rule states that what you say to your own teammates is no longer off-limits to TOs. Will this stop trash talking?

entry.title: Brain-inspired nanopore device uses current-induced heating for memory operations
entry.summary: Some researchers are leaning into biology for inspiration in computing. In particular, neuromorphic computing offers a brain-inspired approach to hardware that replaces traditional binary processing with systems that function more like neurons and synapses. Now, a new study, published in Nature Communications, describes an innovative design for a fluidic memristor that uses its own self-heating mechanism to induce a history-dependent memory effect.
